text: A six-centimeter transumbilical incision is made, followed by the insertion of a hand port and three working trocars into the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon detaches adhesions from the spleen and immobilizes the splenic flexure, with the peritoneum visible on the medial side.
The surgeon mobilizes the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and clips it at its origin, followed by mobilization and clipping of the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) just below the pancreatic border.
The surgeon mobilizes the posterior side of the mesorectum and dissects the anterior side, carefully separating the mesorectal tissue from surrounding structures.
The surgeon transects the rectum using a linear stapler, then removes the bowel segment through the hand port.
The surgeon performs an anastomosis using a circular stapler, and a leak test is conducted, showing no leakage.
The surgical wounds are closed, and the excised specimen is shown.
